crash_type: KASAN out-of-bounds access (use-after-free or heap buffer overflow)

hypotheses.root_cause_hypothesis: In selinux_xfrm_alloc_user, a memcpy operation copies data from a user-controlled security context (uctx) without proper bounds checking, leading to a heap buffer overflow when the context length exceeds the allocated buffer size.
evidence: KASAN report shows check_memory_region_inline -> memcpy -> selinux_xfrm_alloc_user at security/selinux/xfrm.c:99.
The crash occurs in memcpy, indicating an invalid memory access during copy.
The call path includes copy_from_user_sec_ctx -> security_xfrm_policy_alloc -> selinux_xfrm_alloc_user, suggesting user-supplied data is being copied into a kernel buffer.
The source code snippet shows verify_newpolicy_info is called, but no explicit length validation for the security context is visible before the memcpy.
hypotheses.repair_idea: Add a bounds check in selinux_xfrm_alloc_user to ensure that the length of the user-provided security context does not exceed the allocated buffer size before calling memcpy.
hypotheses.uncertainty: Medium: The exact line 99 in selinux_xfrm_alloc_user is not provided, but the stack trace strongly implicates a memcpy from user-controlled data.

hypotheses.root_cause_hypothesis: The function copy_from_user_sec_ctx (inlined in xfrm_policy_construct) fails to validate the size of the security context string (ctx_len) against the actual allocated space, allowing a crafted netlink message to cause a buffer overflow during the subsequent memcpy in selinux_xfrm_alloc_user.
evidence: Stack trace: xfrm_policy_construct -> copy_from_user_sec_ctx -> security_xfrm_policy_alloc -> selinux_xfrm_alloc_user -> memcpy.
copy_from_user_sec_ctx is inlined at net/xfrm/xfrm_user.c:1462, suggesting it handles user context copying logic.
The crash is triggered via xfrm_add_acquire, which processes user-supplied netlink messages, a common attack vector for size mismatches.
hypotheses.repair_idea: In copy_from_user_sec_ctx, validate that ctx_len is within the bounds of the allocated skb or policy structure before passing it to security_xfrm_policy_alloc.
hypotheses.uncertainty: Medium: The exact implementation of copy_from_user_sec_ctx is not shown, but it is the immediate caller of the allocation function.

hypotheses.root_cause_hypothesis: xfrm_policy_construct may incorrectly calculate or pass an oversized length for the security context to downstream functions, possibly due to integer overflow or missing validation of netlink attributes, leading to a heap overflow in selinux_xfrm_alloc_user.
evidence: xfrm_policy_construct is the top-level function in the crash path that processes user input (net/xfrm/xfrm_user.c:1627).
The crash occurs during xfrm_add_acquire, which calls xfrm_policy_construct, indicating the malformed policy is constructed from user data.
The source snippet shows verify_newpolicy_info is called, but this may not cover the security context length.
hypotheses.repair_idea: Add strict length validation for the security context attribute in xfrm_policy_construct before invoking security_xfrm_policy_alloc.
hypotheses.uncertainty: High: The exact point of length miscalculation is unclear without seeing the full xfrm_policy_construct code.
